expectations.text: Uses len(relation) to count without fetching all rows
expectations.evidence: Code: `len(Electrode & {"nwb_file_name": "j1620210710_.nwb"})`; explicitly notes 'DataJoint translates that to a SELECT COUNT(*) on the server, so no rows are fetched'.

expectations.text: Does not fetch the full Electrode set just to take its length
expectations.evidence: Calls out 'len(rel.fetch()) ... pulls every row over the wire just to take its length. Avoid' — actively warns against the anti-pattern.

expectations.text: Bonus: notes that each Electrode row is one recording contact (keyed by electrode_id within an ElectrodeGroup), so the count returns total contacts — not tetrodes/shanks/ElectrodeGroups (e.g., a 32-tetrode session yields 128 Electrode rows, not 32)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'Each row is one recording contact ... not a tetrode' and gives the 32-tetrode → 128 Electrode example.
